title: AI-assisted coding for casual developers
author: Karl Mochel
text:
Member-only story
PRACTICAL DESIGN INNOVATION
AI-assisted coding for casual developers
Lessons from a casual coder using ChatGPT, VSCode Copilot, and Claude.ai to develop a web app.
When I set out to create a tool for 3D printing letter stamps for my daughter, I wasn’t aiming to write perfect code or reinvent the wheel. I wanted a functional web app that could quickly generate text with precise kerning and alignments in various fonts. It seemed like the perfect opportunity to leverage AI tools.
As a casual coder with a background in computer science but limited experience in production coding, I thought these tools could help fill the gaps in my expertise. While they proved invaluable in many ways — writing code and speeding up development — they also introduced challenges. Miscommunications, regressions, and debugging hurdles turned this project into a crash course in collaborating with AI tools effectively.
I was able to use ChatGPT with some help from Claude and VSCode Copilot to develop the web app I wanted, but I still needed some basic HTML/CSS/JS coding/debugging skills to complete it.
You can check out the final page here and use it to understand some of the issues mentioned below. The Project section explains the steps I took. The Postmortem…
